svg.title: Final structure with automatic notes handoff and a manager approval gate
svg.desc: Application tier: Zoom RTMS streams a live speaker-labeled transcript to Flask with SocketIO, which pushes text to the live transcript panel. When the meeting ends, the notes are sent automatically to LLM extraction, which drafts version 1 tasks. A manager review step approves, edits, or rejects the drafts. Approved or edited tasks are written to the SQLite database, and only then does distribution send Google Calendar invites and SendGrid emails. Dashboards read the database with role-based access and a daily scheduler queries it for overdue tasks. The database shows five tables: users, tasks, blockers, jobs, and optional notifications. Below sits the infrastructure tier: Docker, Render, Redis only if multi-worker, AWS later and optional.

g.text: Infrastructure tier: when each is used
